Let $n$ be a positive integer, let $A, B$ be square symmetric $n \times n$
matrices with real entries (so if $a_{ij}$ are the entries of $A$, the $a_{ij}$
are real numbers and $a_{ij} = a_{ji}$). Suppose there are $n \times n$
matrices $X, Y$ (with complex entries) such that $\det(AX + BY) \ne 0$. Prove
that $\det(A^2 + B^2) \ne 0$ ($\det$ indicates the determinant).
